content.text: Overview of all physical connections on Circuit Tracks and how to use them effectively.
The USB-C connection serves multiple purposes:

items: Power: Provides bus power from computer, wall adapter, or USB battery pack
MIDI: Class-compliant USB MIDI (no drivers needed)
Components: Connects to Novation Components for patch editing, sample management, and backup
Firmware: Updates the device firmware when available

content.text: Circuit Tracks features full-size 5-pin DIN MIDI ports for reliable hardware connectivity. Standard MIDI cables connect directly - no adapters required.

items: MIDI In: Receive notes, clock, and control from external sequencers or controllers
MIDI Out: Send sequences to external synths and drum machines
MIDI Thru: Pass incoming MIDI to additional devices in a chain

content.title: Daisy Chaining
content.text: Use the MIDI Thru port to send incoming MIDI to additional devices. This is useful when controlling Circuit Tracks from a DAW while also sending to other hardware.
Circuit Tracks can be powered in two ways:

rows: 5V USB power from computer, wall adapter, or battery pack
6x AA batteries for fully portable operation

content.title: Battery Life
content.text: With good quality AA batteries, expect 4-6 hours of use. Rechargeable NiMH batteries work well and are more economical for regular use.
The 3.5mm sync port sends and receives analog clock signals for synchronizing with modular systems, Korg Volcas, Pocket Operators, and other gear that uses analog clock.

items: Connect USB-C for power (or insert 6x AA batteries)
Connect headphones to the 3.5mm headphone jack
Press the power button to turn on
Adjust Master Volume and start making music
Connect USB-C for power
Connect 1/4" cables from Output L/Mono and Output R to powered speakers or a mixer
Start at low master volume and increase gradually
Connect power via USB-C
Connect a 5-pin DIN MIDI cable from MIDI Out to your external synth's MIDI In
Set external synth to receive on the appropriate MIDI channel
Select a synth track (ch 1 or 2) or MIDI track (ch 3 or 4) on Circuit Tracks
Connect USB-C to computer
Open your DAW and enable Circuit Tracks as a MIDI device
Connect 1/4" audio outputs to your audio interface
Use Circuit Tracks for sequencing soft synths or as a MIDI controller

content.text: Circuit Tracks does not function as a USB audio interface. Audio must be routed separately via the analog 1/4" outputs to your audio interface or mixer.

items: Connect external audio source to Input 1 or Input 2 (1/4" cables)
The external audio appears in the mixer and can be processed through delay/reverb
Adjust input level in the mixer view
Connect outputs to speakers or audio interface
